purpose: Fill this JSON and give it to an AI so it can generate a new pathogenic-protein-cellular-interactions.config.js file for the simulator.
instructions_for_ai: Use this JSON as the source of truth.
Return a complete JavaScript config file that assigns the final object to window.PROTEIN_SIM_CONFIG.
Preserve the same high-level shape used by the simulator: protein, assemblies, and components.
Keep values educational and clearly non-clinical unless validated sources are provided separately.

protein_request.intro_name: Explore a simplified 3D model of alpha-synuclein
protein_request.contact_map_explanation: Explain what the contact map suggests for this protein in simple language.
protein_request.speed_explanation: Explain that motion speed is illustrative rather than a physical rate constant.

interaction_focus.notes: Favor a mitochondria-centered narrative for this protein.

membrane.label: Plasma membrane
membrane.narrative: Describe how this protein engages the plasma membrane.

receptor.label: Synaptic receptor cluster
receptor.narrative: Describe how this protein affects receptor-rich zones.

mitochondria.label: Mitochondrion
mitochondria.narrative: Describe how this protein contributes to mitochondrial stress or dysfunction.

lysosome.label: Lysosome
lysosome.narrative: Describe how this protein burdens clearance pathways.

tau.label: Tau-rich microtubules
tau.narrative: Describe whether cross-talk with tau is relevant for this protein.

also_include: one-paragraph explanation of component affinity choices
